steps.title: Identify the defining contrast
steps.content: ”Less deliberately signaled” describes 1920s–1960s texts, so 1970s feminist writing had politics that were deliberately and openly expressed.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
”Overtly” conveys open, unambiguous expression—what the contrast demands of 1970s feminist writing.

The other three lack this meaning: “prudently” signals caution, “cordially” signals friendliness, and “inadvertently” means unintentional, the opposite of deliberate.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
